title: Siddharth Singh
text: I am a Statistics and Data Science student at UCLA with a minor in Data Science Engineering, interested in machine learning, data engineering, and applied analytics. My work spans research, data journalism, and industry projects in healthcare, sports, and automotive systems.

title: Siddharth Singh
text:
I am a UCLA student pursuing a B.S. in Statistics and Data Science with a minor in Data Science Engineering, expected to graduate in June 2026. I am especially interested in machine learning, predictive modeling, data engineering, and building analytical tools that are both technically rigorous and practically useful.
My experience spans academic research, data journalism, and industry analytics. Across these settings, I enjoy working on problems that require careful modeling, clear communication, and thoughtful interpretation of data.

title: Siddharth Singh
text:
Highlights
Lucid Motors: Built Python automation and validation tools for OTA software workflows, improving efficiency and speeding validation.
UCLA Bionics Lab: Developed deep learning and data augmentation pipelines for automated stroke assessment using multimodal sensor data.
Bruin Sports Analytics: Lead data journalism and research projects using statistical modeling and machine learning for sports analysis.

title: Projects
text:
At the UCLA Bionics Lab, I worked on deep learning models for automated stroke assessment using time-series sensor and computer vision data. My contributions included evaluating CNN, LSTM, and hybrid architectures, implementing subject-wise data splitting to avoid leakage, and experimenting with augmentation methods such as jittering, interpolation, and conditional GANs.
Student Research Program Report

title: Projects
section: Predicting Norwegian Residential EV Charging Loads
text:
Predicting Norwegian Residential EV Charging Loads
Built a PyTorch sequential model to predict residential EV charging demand, outperforming regression baselines and improving forecasting for utility planning.

title: Projects
text:
Uber Fare Prediction
Implemented a feature-engineered XGBoost regression model for fare prediction, achieving a low RMSE and improving pricing accuracy.

title: Projects
text:
Connect N Game
Built a C++ game engine with multiple classes and a minimax-based SmartPlayer, focusing on efficient game-tree search and optimized response time.

title: Experience
text:
Newark, CA | June 2025 – September 2025
Built Python-based automation frameworks for OTA software validation, integrating REST APIs, Jama, and GitLab CI to improve workflow efficiency. Developed ECU validation tools that generated vehicle-level PASS/FAIL reports and accelerated validation speed, while also creating Tableau dashboards to summarize release performance and issues.

title: Experience
text:
UCLA Bionics Lab — Research Assistant
Los Angeles, CA | February 2025 – Present
Develop deep learning and transfer learning pipelines for automated stroke assessment using 10,000+ multimodal sensor and computer vision samples. Implemented data augmentation and K-fold cross-validation systems that improved model evaluation and increased pretrain testing accuracy by 30%. I also contributed to a UCLA Student Research Program report on automated Fugl-Meyer Assessment and augmentation methods.

title: Experience
section: Bruin Sports Analytics — Research Lead & Data Journalism Chair
text:
Bruin Sports Analytics — Research Lead & Data Journalism Chair
Los Angeles, CA | September 2024 – Present
Lead research and mentor undergraduate writers on sports analytics projects. My work has included applying Lasso regression, PCA, XGBoost, and clustering methods to problems in basketball and soccer, along with co-authoring published sports analytics articles.

title: Experience
text:
Daily Bruin — Data Journalist
Los Angeles, CA | January 2024 – Present
Create data-driven stories and interactive visualizations using long-term institutional datasets. Projects have examined UCLA dining and pest-control spending, Greek life performance metrics, and faculty employment trends using large administrative datasets and interview-based reporting.

title: Experience
text:
Premise Health — Data Analytics Intern
Brentwood, TN | June 2023 – September 2023
Analyzed pharmacy claims and utilization data to identify spending patterns, estimate savings from onsite pharmacy access, and surface operational insights relevant to ROI and patient support.
